local_names.romanized_local: Jeera Rice / Zeera Rice / Cumin Rice

food_subgroup: Cooked basmati rice tempered with cumin and ghee

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Jeera Rice. Nearest parent is a cooked rice dish with spice tempering. A new concept for Indian cumin-tempered rice preparations is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cooked basmati rice with cumin tempering (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of jeera rice is 1 bowl (approximately 200g) — fluffy, separate-grain cooked basmati rice fragrant with toasted cumin seeds and a subtle ghee aroma.

nutrient_source.title: Jeera Rice — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, derived from basmati rice and ghee composition data
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN does not have a standalone jeera rice entry. Values derived from ICMR-NIN cooked basmati rice data plus standard tempering proportions (approximately 2g ghee and 0.5g cumin per 100g cooked rice). Cumin seeds add negligible calories at this proportion.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — cooked basmati rice GI approximately 58; the ghee and cumin tempering slightly lower GI vs plain boiled rice. Basmati has a lower GI than regular long-grain white rice.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 290mg per bowl (200g) — low to moderate. Jeera rice is naturally low in sodium.
hypertension.guidance: Suitable for hypertensive patients. One of the lower-sodium rice preparations. Cumin seeds may have mild blood pressure-lowering effects in regular dietary quantities.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~58) — lower than regular white rice; glycaemic load per bowl (200g) is approximately 37. The basmati rice GI is meaningfully lower than standard white rice (~73).
type2_diabetes.guidance: Jeera rice is a better choice than regular rice for diabetic patients due to basmati's lower GI. Limit to 150g portions and pair with high-protein, high-fibre curries to further moderate the glycaemic response.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Cumin has documented anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties; ghee provides butyrate and fat-soluble vitamins.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis — the cumin provides anti-inflammatory compounds and the ghee provides beneficial fat-soluble nutrients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jeera Rice is a simple, aromatic North Indian rice dish — long-grain aged basmati rice cooked by the absorption method to achieve individual, separate, fluffy grains, then finished with a quick tempering of ghee and whole cumin seeds (jeera) that are crackled in hot ghee until fragrant. The result is a perfectly seasoned rice with a subtle nutty-earthy cumin fragrance. Served as the plain rice accompaniment to Indian curries at virtually every North Indian restaurant. Basmati rice has a lower GI than regular white rice, making it a slightly better choice for blood glucose management.

confidence_reason: No standalone jeera rice entry in ICMR-NIN or HPB Foodo. Values derived from ICMR-NIN cooked basmati rice data plus standard tempering (ghee and cumin) proportions. Basmati rice composition is well-established.
notes: Contains dairy (ghee) — not vegan or dairy-free when made with ghee. Vegan when made with oil. Cholesterol approximately 15mg per bowl (200g) when made with ghee; 0.0 when made with oil. Cumin seeds at the standard proportion (~0.5g per 100g cooked rice) contribute negligible additional calories.
